title: Trevor Darrell | EECS at UC Berkeley
content: Trevor Darrell | EECS at UC Berkeley Trevor Darrell: Faculty Home Page Larger Photo Trevor Darrell Professor in Residence Contact Information 8010 Berkeley Way West trevor@eecs.berkeley.edu Links Personal Homepage Dissertations Research Support Taylor Kaserman 776 Soda taylor.kase@berkeley.edu Biography Professor Darrell is on the faculty of the CS Division at UC Berkeley. His group develops algorithms to enable visual recognition across a variety of platforms and applications. His interests include computer vision, machine learning, computer graphics, and perception-based human computer interfaces. Prof. Darrell was on the faculty of the MIT EECS department from 1999-2008, where he directed the Vision Interface Group. He was a member of the research staff at Interval Research Corporation from 1996-1999, and received the S.M. and Ph.D. degrees from MIT in 1992 and 1996, respectively. He obtained the B.S.E. degree from the University of Pennsylvania in 1988, having started his career in computer vision as an undergraduate researcher in Ruzena Bajcsyâs GRASP lab. Education 1996, PhD, MAS, MIT 1988, BSE, CS, U.Penn. Research Areas Artificial Intelligence (AI) Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR) Computer Vision Research Centers Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR) Berkeley Center for Responsible, Decentralized Intelligence (RDI) (RDI) Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD) CITRIS People and Robots (CPAR) International Computer Science Institute (ICSI) Partners for Advanced Transit and Highways (PATH) Teaching Schedule CS 294-194.
